title: Quantitative Solubility Method for Studying Reversible One : One Complex Formation in Non-ideal Solutions
author: Ram D. Srivastava
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Here is reported a solubility method for studying one : one charge transfer complex formation in non-ideal solutions. Formation constants and the heats of formation for the system nitrobenzene and some aro­matic hydrocarbon donors have been calculated using this method. The values calculated for iodine-benzine and iodine-ether from the solubility data are in good agreement with spectrophotometric value. The results indicate that weak charge transfer complexes are formed because of specific interaction between in­dividual hydrocarbon donor and nitrobenzene molecules
steps_raw: Here is reported a solubility method for studying one : one charge transfer complex formation in non-ideal solutions. The values calculated for iodine-benzine and iodine-ether from the solubility data are in good agreement with spectrophotometric value. The results indicate that weak charge transfer complexes are formed because of specific interaction between in­dividual hydrocarbon donor and nitrobenzene molecules
